title: viburnum opulus var. subintegrifolium
intro: Viburnum opulus var. subintegrifolium, also known as the Downy Hawthorn or Small-leaved Cranberrybush, is a variety of the Guelder Rose plant, characterized by its smaller leaves and white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and relieving menstrual cramps due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus var. subintegrifolium include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat various health issues and skin conditions.
